verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I Confess to you, holy brethren, I was afraid the cold would have made you cold in assembling yourselves together; but since you prove by this, your crowded assembly, that you are fervent in spirit, I doubt not that you have also prayed for me, that I may pay you what I owe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I promised you in the name of Christ that, as the shortness of the time prevented us from expounding it before, I would to-day discuss why God was pleased to manifest the Holy Ghost in the form of a dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That this may be explained, this day has dawned on us; and I perceive that from eagerness to hear, and pious devotion, you have come together in greater number than usual.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May God, by our mouth, fulfill your expectation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For your coming together is of your love; but love of what?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If of us, even that is well; for we desire to be loved by you, but not in ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because we love you in Christ, do you love us in Christ in return, and let our love mutually sigh towards God; for the note of the dove is a sighing or moaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now if the dove’s note is a moaning, as we all know it to be, and doves moan in love, hear what the apostle says, and wonder not that the Holy Ghost willed to be manifested in the form of a dove: “For what we should pray for as we ought,” says he, “we know not; but the Spirit Himself intercedes for us with groanings which cannot be uttered.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, my brethren? shall we say this, that the Spirit groans where He has perfect and eternal blessedness with the Father and the Son?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Holy Spirit is God, even as the Son of God is God, and the Father God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have said “God” thrice, but not three Gods; for indeed it is God thrice rather than three Gods; because the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost are one God: this you know full well.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is not then in Himself with Himself in that Trinity, in that blessedness, in that His eternal substance, that the Holy Spirit groans; but in us He groans because He makes us to groan.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor is it a little matter that the Holy Spirit teaches us to groan, for He gives us to know that we are sojourners in a foreign land, and He teaches us to sigh after our native country; and through that very longing do we groan.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He with whom it is well in this world, or rather he who thinks it is well with him, who exults in the joy of carnal things, in the abundance of things temporal, in an empty felicity, has the cry of the raven; for the raven’s cry is full of clamor, not of groaning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But he who knows that he is in the pressure of this mortal life, a pilgrim “absent from the Lord,” that he does not yet possess that perpetual blessedness which is promised to us, but that he has it in hope, and will have it in reality when the Lord shall come openly in glory who came before in humility concealed; he, I say, who knows this doth groan.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so long as it is for this he groans, he does well to groan; it was the Spirit that taught him to groan, he learnt it from the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many indeed groan by reason of earthly misery.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are shattered, it may be, by losses, or weighed down by bodily ailment, or shut up in prisons, or bound with chains, or tossed about on the waves of the sea, or hedged in by the ensnaring devices of their enemies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore do they groan, but not with the moaning of the dove, not with love of God, not in the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, when such are delivered from these same afflictions, they exult with loud voices, whereby it is made manifest that they are ravens, not doves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was with good reason that a raven was sent forth from the ark, and returned not again; a dove was sent forth, and it returned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These two birds Noah sent forth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had there the raven, and also the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That ark contained both kinds; and if the ark was a figure of the Church, you see indeed that in the present deluge of the world, the Church must of necessity contain both kinds, as well the raven as the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are the ravens?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who seek their own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are the doves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who seek the things that are Christ’s.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, when He sent the Holy Spirit He manifested Him visibly in two ways—by a dove and by fire: by a dove upon the Lord when He was baptized, by fire upon the disciples when they were gathered together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For when the Lord had ascended into heaven after His resurrection, having spent forty days with His disciples, and the day of Pentecost being fully come, He sent unto them the Holy Spirit as He had promised.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly the Spirit coming at that time filled the place, and there was first a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty wind, as we read in the Acts of the Apostles, and “there appeared unto them,” it says, “cloven tongues as of fire, and it sat upon each of them; and they began to speak with tongues, as the Spirit gave them utterance.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here we have seen a dove descending upon the Lord; there, cloven tongues upon the assembled disciples: in the former, simplicity is shown; in the latter, fervency.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now there are who are said to be simple, who are only indolent; they are called simple, but they are only slow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not such was Stephen, full of the Holy Ghost: he was simple, because he injured no one; he was fervent, because he reproved the ungodly.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he held not his peace before the Jews.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His are those burning words: “Ye stiff-necked and uncircumcised of heart and ears, ye do always resist the Holy Spirit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mighty impetuosity; but it is the dove without gall raging.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For that you know that he was fierce without gall, see how, upon hearing these words, they who were the ravens immediately took up stones and rushed together upon this dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They begin to stone Stephen; and he who a little before stormed and glowed with ardor of spirit,—who had, as it were, made an onset on his enemies, and like one full of violence had attacked them in such fiery and burning words as you have heard, “Ye stiff-necked and uncircumcised in heart and ears,” that any one who heard those words might fancy that Stephen, if he were allowed, would have them consumed at once,—but when the stones thrown from their hands reached him, with fixed knee he saith, “Lord, lay not this sin to their charge.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He held fast to the unity of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For his Master, upon whom the dove descended, had done the same thing before him; who, while hanging on the cross, said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore by the dove it is shown that they who are sanctified by the Spirit should be without guile; and that their simplicity should not continue cold is shown us by the fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor let it trouble you that the tongues were divided; for tongues are diverse, therefore the appearance was that of cloven tongues.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Cloven tongues,” it saith, “as of fire, and it sat upon each of them.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a diversity of tongues, but the diversity of tongues does not imply schisms.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be not afraid of separation in the cloven tongues; in the dove recognize unity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence in this manner it behoved the Holy Spirit to be manifested when coming upon the Lord, that every one might understand that if he has the Holy Spirit he ought to be simple as the dove, to have true peace with his brethren, that peace which the kisses of doves signify.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ravens have their kisses too; but in the case of the ravens it is a false peace, in that of the dove a true peace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not every one, therefore, who says, “Peace be with you,” is to be listened to as if he were a dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then are the kisses of ravens distinguished from those of doves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ravens kiss, but they tear; the nature of doves is innocent of tearing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where consequently there is tearing, there is not true peace in the kisses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They have true peace who have not torn the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ravens feed upon carrion, it is not so with the dove; it lives on the fruits of the earth, its food is innocent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, brethren, is really worthy of admiration in the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sparrows are very small birds, but yet they kill flies at least.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove does nothing of this sort, for it does not feed on what is dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who have torn the Church feed on the dead.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: God is mighty; let us pray that they who are devoured by them, and perceive it not, may come to life again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many acknowledge that they do come to life again, for at their coming we daily express joy with them in the name of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be ye simple, but only in such wise that ye be fervent, and let your fervor be in your tongues.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hold not your peace, speak with glowing tongues, set those that are cold on fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For why, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who does not see what they do not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And no wonder; for they who are unwilling to return from that are just like the raven that was sent forth from the ark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who does not see what they see not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are unthankful even to the Holy Spirit Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, the dove descended upon the Lord, upon the Lord when baptized: and thereupon was manifested that holy and real Trinity, which to us is one God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Lord went up out of the water, as we read in the Gospel: “And, lo, the heavens were opened unto Him, and He saw the Spirit descending like a dove, and it abode upon Him: and immediately a voice followed, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Trinity most manifestly appears: the Father in the voice, the Son in the man, the Spirit in the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this Trinity let us see, as we do see, whereunto the apostles were sent forth, and what it is wonderful those men do not see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not indeed that they really do not see, but that they really shut their eyes to that which strikes them in the very face: that whereunto the disciples were sent forth in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost, by Him of whom it is said, “This is He that baptizeth:” it was said, in fact, to His ministers, by Him who has retained this authority to Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now this it was in Him that John saw, and came to know which he did not know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that he did not know Him to be the Son of God, or that he did not know Him to be the Lord, or not know Him to be the Christ; or that he did not know this too, that it was He who should baptize with water and with the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This he did know; but that he should do this so as to retain the authority to Himself and transfer it to none of His ministers, this is what he learnt in the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by this authority, which Christ has retained to Himself alone, and conferred upon none of His ministers, though He has deigned to baptize by His ministers; by this authority, I say, stands the unity of the Church, which is figured in the dove, concerning which it is said, “My dove is one, the only one of her mother.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if, as I have already said, my brethren, the authority were transferred by the Lord to His minister, there would be as many baptisms as ministers, and the unity of baptism would no longer exist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mark, brethren; before our Lord Jesus Christ came to His baptism (for it was after the baptism that the dove descended, whereby John recognized something that was peculiar to Him, since he was told, “Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending like a dove, and remaining on Him, the same is He that baptizeth with the Holy Ghost”), John knew that He it was that baptizeth with the Holy Ghost; but that it should be with this peculiarity, that the authority should not pass from Him to another, notwithstanding He confers it, this is what he learnt there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence do we prove that John did already know that the Lord was to baptize with the Holy Ghost; so that what he must be understood to have learned by the dove is, that the Lord was to baptize with the Holy Ghost in such wise that the authority should not pass from Him to any other man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence do we prove this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove descended after the Lord was baptized; but before the Lord came to be baptized by John in the Jordan, we have said that John knew Him, on the evidence of those words, in which he says, “Comest Thou to me to be baptized?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have need to be baptized of Thee.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, he did know Him to be the Lord, knew Him to be the Son of God; how do we prove that he knew already that the same was He who should baptize with the Holy Ghost?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before He came to the river, whilst many people were running together to John to be baptized, he says to them, “I indeed baptize you with water; but He that cometh after me is greater than I, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to loose; the same shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with fire.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already he knew this also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then did he learn from the dove, that he may not afterwards be found a liar (which God forbid we should think), if it be not this, that there was to be a certain peculiarity in Christ, such that, although many ministers, be they righteous or unrighteous, should baptize, the virtue of baptism would be attributed to Him alone on whom the dove descended, and of whom it was said, “This is He that baptizeth with the Holy Ghost”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter may baptize, but this is He that baptizeth; Paul may baptize, yet this is He that baptizeth; Judas may baptize, still this is He that baptizeth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the sanctity of baptism be according to the diversity of merits in them that administer it, then as merits are diverse there will be diverse baptisms; and the recipient will imagine that what he receives is so much the better, the better he appears to be from whom he received it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The saints themselves—understand brethren, they that belong to the dove, that have their part in that city of Jerusalem, the good themselves in the Church, of whom the apostle says, “The Lord know eth them that are His”—are endued with different graces, and do not all possess like merits.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some are more holy than others, some are better than others.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore if one receive baptism from him, for example, who is a righteous saint, another from another who is of inferior merit with God, of inferior degree, of inferior continence, of inferior life, how notwithstanding is that which they receive one, equal and like, if it be not because, “This is He that baptizeth”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just, then, as when the good and the better administer baptism, one man does not receive a good thing, another a better; but, notwithstanding that the ministers were one good the other better, they receive what is one and equal, not a better in the one case and a worse in the other; so, too, when a bad man administers baptism, through the ignorance or forbearance of the Church (for bad men either are not known as such, or are borne with; the chaff is tolerated until the floor be fully purged at the last), that which is given is one, not unlike because the ministers are unlike, but like and equal because “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, beloved, let us see what those men desire not to see; not what they may not see, but what they grieve to see, as though it were shut against them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither were the disciples sent to baptize as ministers, in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither were they sent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Go,” said He, “baptize the nations.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard, brethren, how that inheritance comes, “Ask of me, and I will give Thee the nations for Thine inheritance, and the utmost bounds of the earth for Thy possessions.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You have heard how that “from Sion went forth the law, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was there the disciples were told, “Go, baptize the nations in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We became attentive when we heard, “Go, baptize the nations.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In whose name?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is one God; for it says not in the “names” of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost, but “in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Where thou hearest one name, there is one God; just as it was said of Abraham’s seed, and the Apostle Paul expounds it, “In thy seed shall all nations be blessed; he said not, In seeds, as in many, but as in one, and in thy seed which is Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore, just as the apostle wished to show thee that, because in that place it is not said “in seeds,” Christ is one; so here too, when it is said, “in the name,” not in the names, even as these, “in seed,” not in seeds, is it proved that the Father, and the Son, and the Holy Ghost are one God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But lo, say the disciples to the Lord, we are told in what name we are to baptize; Thou hast made us ministers, and hast said to us, “Go, baptize in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither shall we go?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have you not heard?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To Mine inheritance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You ask, Whither shall we go?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To that which I bought with my blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the nations, saith He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I fancied that He said, Go, baptize the Africans in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thanks be to God, the Lord has solved the question the dove has taught us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thanks be to God, it was to the nations the apostles were sent; if to the nations, then to all tongues.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Holy Spirit signified this, being divided in the tongues, united in the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here the tongues are divided, there the dove unites them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The tongues of the nations agreed, perhaps that of Africa alone disagreed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can be more evident, my brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the dove the unity, in the tongues the community of the nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For once the tongues became discordant through pride, and then of one became many tongues.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after the flood certain proud men, as if endeavoring to fortify themselves against God, as if aught were high for God, or aught could give security to pride, raised a tower, apparently that they might not be destroyed by a flood, should there come one thereafter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they had heard and considered that all iniquity was swept away by a flood; to abstain from iniquity they would not; they sought the height of a tower as a defense against a flood; they built a lofty tower.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “God saw their pride, and frustrated their purpose by causing that they should not understand one another’s speech, and thus tongues became diverse through pride.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If pride caused diversities of tongues, Christ’s humility has united these diversities in one.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Church is now bringing together what that tower had sundered.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of one tongue there were made many; marvel not: this was the doing of pride.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of many tongues there is made one; marvel not: this was the doing of charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For although the sounds of tongues are various, in the heart one God is invoked, one peace preserved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then should the Holy Spirit have been manifested when signifying a unity, if not by the dove, so that it might be said to the Church brought into a state of peace, “My dove is one”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How ought humility to have been represented but by an innocent, sorrowing bird; not by a proud, exulting bird like the raven?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps they will say: Well, as it is a dove, and the dove is one, baptism there cannot be apart from the one dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore if the dove is with thee, or if thou be thyself a dove, do thou give me, when I come to thee, that which I have not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know that this is what they say; but you will presently see that it is not of the voice of the dove, but of the clamor of the raven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For attend a little, beloved, and fear their devices; nay, beware of them, and listen to the words of gainsayers only to reject them, not to swallow them and take them into your bowels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do therein what the Lord did when they offered Him the bitter draught, “He tasted, and spat it out;” so also you hear and cast away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What indeed say they?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Lo, says he, “Thou art the dove.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O Catholic Church, it is to thee it is said, “My dove is one, the only one of her mother,” to thee certainly is it said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Stop, do not question me; prove first whether to me it was said; if it was said to me, I would hear it at once.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To thee,” saith he, “it was said.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I answer, in the voice of the Catholic Church, “To me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this answer, brethren, sounding forth from my mouth alone, has sounded, as I believe, also from your hearts, and we all affirmed together, yea, to the Catholic Church was it said, “One is my dove, the only one of her mother.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Apart from this dove, says he further, there is no baptism: I was baptized apart from this dove, consequently have not baptism; if I have not baptism, why dost thou not give it me when I come to thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I also will put questions; let us meanwhile lay aside the inquiry as to whom this was said, “My dove is one, the only one of her mother;”—as yet we are inquiring;—it was said either to me or to thee; let us postpone the question as to whom it was said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is what I ask, if the dove is simple, innocent, without gall, peaceful in its kisses, not fierce with its talons, I ask whether the covetous, the rapacious, the crafty, the sottish, the infamous, belong to the members of this dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are they members of this dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far be the thought, says he.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who would really say this, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To speak of nothing else, if I mention the rapacious alone, members of the hawk they may be, not members of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Kites seize and plunder, so do hawks, so do ravens; doves do not plunder nor tear, consequently they who snatch and rob are not members of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was there not even one rapacious person among you?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why abides the baptism, which in this case the hawk, not the dove, has given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do you not among yourselves baptize after robbers, after adulterers, after drunkards?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why not baptize after the avaricious among yourselves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Are these all members of the dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You so dishonor your dove that you make those that have the nature of the vulture her members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, brethren, what say we?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are the bad and the good in the Catholic Church, but with them the bad only.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps I say this with a hostile feeling: let this too be afterwards examined.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They do say, certainly, that among them are the good and the bad; for, should they assert that they have only the good, let their own credit it, and I subscribe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With us, let them say, there are none but holy, righteous, chaste, sober men; no adulterers, no usurers, no deceivers, no false swearers, no wine-bibbers;—let them say this, for I heed not their tongues, I touch their hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since they are well known to us, and to you, and to their own, just as you are known both to yourselves in the Catholic Church and to them, neither let us find fault with them, nor let them flatter themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We confess that in the Church there are good and bad, yet as the grain and the chaff.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Sometimes he who is baptized by the grain is chaff, and he who is baptized by the chaff is grain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Otherwise, if his baptism who is baptized by the grain stands good, and his who is baptized by the chaff not, then it is not true, “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if it is true “This is He that baptizeth,” then what is given by the chaff stands good, and he baptizeth in like manner as the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the bad man (who administers baptism) is not the dove, nor belongs to the members of the dove, nor can he possibly be affirmed to be so, either with us in the Catholic Church or with them, if they assert that their Church is the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then are we to understand, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since it is evident, and known to all, and they must admit, though it be against their will, that when with them bad men give baptism, it is not given after those bad men; and with us, too, when the bad give baptism, it is not given after them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove does not baptize after the raven; why then would the raven baptize after the dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider, beloved, why also was there a something pointed out by means of the dove, as that the dove—namely, the Holy Spirit in the shape of a dove—came to the Lord on being baptized, and rested upon Him, whilst by the coming of the dove John learned this, that there dwelt in the Lord a power peculiarly His own to baptize?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because it was by this power peculiar to Himself, as I have said, the peace of the Church was made secure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet it may be that one may have baptism apart from the dove; but that baptism apart from the dove should do him good, is impossible.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider, beloved, and understand what I say, for by this deception they mislead such of our brethren as are dull and cold.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us be more simple and more fervent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, say they, have I received, or have I not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I answer, Thou hast received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, if I have received, there is nothing which thou canst give me; I am safe, even on thine own evidence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For I affirm that I have received, and thou, too, dost confess that I have received: I am safe by the confession of both: what then dost thou promise me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why wouldst thou make me a Catholic, when thou wouldst not give me anything further, seeing thou confessest that I have already received that which thou affirmest thyself to possess?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when I say, Come to me, I say that thou dost not possess, who yet confessest that I do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why dost thou say, Come to me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove teaches us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From the head of the Lord she answers, and says, Thou hast baptism, but the charity with which I groan thou hast not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How is this, says he, I have baptism, and have not charity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have I the sacraments, and not charity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not shout: show me how can he who divides unity have charity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I, saith he, have baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast; but that baptism, without charity, profits thee nothing; because without charity thou art nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The baptism itself, even in him who is nothing, is not nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Baptism, indeed, is something, aye, something great, for His sake, of whom it is said, “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But lest thou shouldst fancy that that which is great can profit thee aught, if thou be not in unity, it was after He was baptized that the dove descended, as if intimating, If thou hast baptism, be in the dove, lest what thou hast profit thee not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come, then, to the dove, we say; not that thou mayest begin to have what thou hadst not before, but that what thou didst have may begin to profit thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou didst have baptism to destruction without; if thou shalt have it within, it begins to profit thee to salvation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For not only was baptism not profitable to thee, and not also hurtful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even holy things may be hurtful.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the good, indeed, holy things are to salvation; in the evil, to judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we certainly know, brethren, what we receive, and what we receive is at any rate holy, and no one says that it is not: and what says the apostle?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But he that eateth and drinketh unworthily, eateth and drinketh judgment to himself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not say that the thing itself is bad, but that the evil man, by receiving it amiss, receives the good thing which he does receive to judgment.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was that morsel which the Lord delivered to Judas evil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The physician would not give poison; it was health the physician gave; but by unworthily receiving it, he who received it not being at peace, received it unto destruction.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So likewise also he who is baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I have (baptism), says he, for myself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast it, I admit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Give good heed to what thou hast; by that very thing which thou hast thou wilt be condemned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because thou hast what belongs to the dove apart from the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast what is the dove’s in the dove, thou art safe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Suppose thyself a soldier: if thou hast thy general’s mark within the lines, thou servest in safety; but if thou hast it out of bounds, not only that mark will not be of advantage to thee for service, but thou wilt even be punished as a deserter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come, then, come, and do not say, I have already, I have enough.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come; the dove is calling thee, calling thee by her sighing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: My brethren, to you I say, call by groaning, not by quarreling; call by praying, by invitation, by fasting; let them by your charity understand that you pity them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I doubt not, my brethren, that if they see your sorrow they will be astonished, and will come to life again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come, then, come; be not afraid; be afraid if thou do not come; nay, be not afraid, rather bewail thyself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come, thou wilt rejoice if thou wilt come; thou wilt indeed groan in the tribulations of thy pilgrimage, but thou wilt rejoice in hope.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come where the dove is, to whom it was said, “My dove is one, the only one of her mother.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou not the one dove upon the head of Christ, seest thou not the tongues throughout the whole world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is the same Spirit by the dove and by the tongues: if by the dove the same Spirit, and by the tongues the same Spirit, then was the Holy Spirit given to the whole world, from which Spirit thou hast cut thyself off, that thou mightest clamor with the raven, not that thou mightest sigh with the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou art anxious, it may be, and sayest, I was baptized without; I fear lest therefore I am guilty, in that I was baptized without.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already thou beginnest to know what thou hast to bewail.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou sayest truly that thou art guilty, not because of thy re ceiving, but because of thy receiving without.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Keep then what thou hast received; amend thy receiving it without.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast received what is the dove’s apart from the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here are two things said to thee: Thou hast received, and, Apart from the dove thou hast received.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that thou hast received, I approve; that thou hast received without, I disapprove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Keep then what thou hast received, it is not changed, but recognized: it is the mark of my king, I will not profane it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I will correct the deserter, not change the mark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Boast not of thy baptism because I call it a real baptism.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, I say that it is so; the whole Catholic Church says that it is so; the dove regards it, and acknowledges it, and groans because thou hast it without; she sees therein what she may acknowledge, sees also what she may correct.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a real baptism, come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou boastest that it is real, and yet wilt thou not come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then of the wicked, who do not belong to the dove?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Saith the dove to thee, Even the wicked, among whom I groan, who belong not to my members, and it must needs be that I groan among them, have not they that which thou boastest of having?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have not many drunkards baptism?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have not many covetous?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have not many idolaters, and, what is worse, who are such by stealth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not the pagans resort, or at least did resort, publicly to idols?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now Christians secretly seek out diviners and consult astrologers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet these have baptism; but the dove groans among ravens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then dost thou boast in the having it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This that thou hast, the wicked man also has.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Have thou humility, charity, peace; have thou the good thing which as yet thou hast not, so that the good thing which thou hast may profit thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what thou hast, even Simon Magus had: the Acts of the Apostles are witness, that canonical book which has to be read in the Church every year.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You know that every year, in the season following the Lord’s Passion, that book is read, wherein it is written, how the apostle was converted, and from a persecutor became a preacher; also, how on the day of Pentecost the Holy Spirit was sent in cloven tongues as of fire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There we read that in Samaria many believed through the preaching of Philip: and he is understood to have been either one of the apostles or one of the deacons; for we read there that seven deacons were ordained, among whom is the name of Philip.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, then, through the preaching of Philip the Samaritans believed; Samaria began to abound in believers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Simon Magus was there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By his magical arts he had so befooled the people, that they fancied him to be the power of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Impressed, however, by the signs which were done by Philip, he also believed; but in what manner he believed, the events that followed afterwards proved.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Simon also was baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostles, who were at Jerusalem, heard this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter and John were sent to those in Samaria; they found many baptized; and as none of them had as yet received the Holy Ghost,—in like manner as He at that time descended, so as that they on whom the Holy Spirit came should speak with tongues, for a manifest token that the nations would believe,—they laid their hands on them, praying for them, and they received the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Simon—who was not a dove but a raven in the Church, because he sought his own things, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s; whence he loved the power which was in the Christians more than the righteousness—Simon, I say, saw that the Holy Spirit was given by the laying on of the hands of the apostles (not that it was given by them, but given in answer to their prayers), and he said to them, “How much money will ye that I give you, so that by the laying on of my hands also, the Holy Ghost may be given?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou thoughtest that the gift of God was to be bought with money.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom said he, “Thy money perish with thee”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Undoubtedly to one that was baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Baptism he had already; but he did not cleave to the bowels of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand that he did not; attend to the very words of the Apostle Peter, for he goes on, “Thou hast no part nor lot in this faith: for I see that thou art in the gall of bitterness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove has no gall; Simon had, and for that reason he was separated from the bowels of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did baptism profit him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not therefore boast of thy baptism, as if that were of itself enough for thy salvation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be not angry, put away thy gall, come to the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here that will profit thee, which without not only did not profit thee, but even was prejudicial to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Neither say, I will not come, because I was baptized without.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, begin to have charity, begin to have fruit, let there be fruit found in thee, and the dove will send thee within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We find this in Scripture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The ark was made of incorruptible wood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The incorruptible timbers are the saints, the faithful that belong to Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as in the temple the living stones of which it is built are said to be faithful men, so likewise the incorruptible timbers are they who persevere in the faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that same ark, then, the timbers were incorruptible.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now the ark is the Church, it is there the dove baptizeth; for the ark was borne on the water, the incorruptible timbers were baptized within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We find that certain timbers were baptized without, such as all the trees that were in the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless the water was the same, not another sort; all had come from heaven, or from abysses of the fountains.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was the same water in which the incorruptible timbers which were in the ark were baptized, and in which the timbers that were without were baptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove was sent forth, and at first found no rest for its feet; it returned to the ark, for all was full of water, and it preferred to return rather than be rebaptized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the raven was sent out before the water was dried up.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rebaptized, it desired not to return, and died in those waters.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: May God avert from us that raven’s death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For why did not the raven return, unless because it was taken off by the waters?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But on the other hand, the dove not finding rest for its feet, whilst the water was crying to it on every side, “Come, come, dip thyself here;” just as these heretics cry, “Come, come, here thou hast it;” the dove, finding no rest for its feet, returned to the ark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Noah sent it out a second time, just as the ark sends you out to speak to them; and what did the dove afterwards?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because there were timbers without that were baptized, it brought back to the ark an olive branch.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That branch had both leaves and fruit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let there not be in thee words only, nor leaves only; let there be fruit, and thou returnest to the ark, not of thyself, the dove calls thee back.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Groan ye without, that ye may call them back within.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, as to this fruit of the olive, if the matter be examined, you will find what it was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The fruit of the olive signifies charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do we prove this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as oil is kept down by no liquid, but bursting through all, bounds up and overtops them; so likewise charity cannot be pressed to the bottom, but must of necessity show itself at the top.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the apostle says of it, “Yet show I unto you a more excellent way.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since we have said of oil that it overtops other liquids, in case it should not be of charity, the apostle said, “I show you a more excellent way,” let us hear what follows.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Go now, Donatus, and cry, “I am eloquent;” go now, and cry, “I am learned.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How far eloquent?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How far learned?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou spoken with the tongues of angels?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet though thou wert to speak with the tongues of angels, not having charity, I should hear only sounding brass and tinkling cymbals.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I want solidity; let me find fruit among the leaves; let there be not words merely, let them have the olive, let them return to the ark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I have the sacrament, thou wilt say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou sayest the truth; the sacrament is divine; thou hast baptism, and that I confess.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what says the apostle?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “If I should know all mysteries, and have prophecy and all faith, so that I could remove mountains;” in case thou shouldest say this, “I believe; enough for me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what says James?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The devils believe and tremble.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Faith is mighty, but without charity it profits nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The devils confessed Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly it was from believing, but not from loving, they said, “What have we to do with Thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They had faith, but not charity; hence they were devils.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Boast not of faith; so far thou art on a level with the devils.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Say not to Christ, What have I to do with Thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ’s unity speaks to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come, learn peace, return to the bowels of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast been baptized without; have fruit, and thou returnest to the ark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But sayest thou, “Why do you seek us if we are bad men?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That you may be good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The reason why we seek you is, because you are bad; for if you were not bad, we should have found you, and would not be seeking you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who is good is already found; he who is bad is still sought after.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consequently, we are seeking you; return ye to the ark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But I have baptism already.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Though I should know all mysteries, and have prophecy and all faith, so as to remove mountains, but have not charity, I am nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let me see fruit there; let me see the olive there, and thou art called back to the ark.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what sayest thou?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Behold, we suffer many evils.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Would that ye suffered these for Christ, not for your own honor!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what follows: They, indeed, boast sometimes, because they do many alms, give to the poor; because they suffer afflictions: but it is for Donatus, not for Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider how thou sufferest; for if thou sufferest for Donatus, it is for a proud man: thou art not in the dove if thou art suffering for Donatus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Donatus was not the friend of the Bridegroom; for had he been, he would have sought the glory of the Bridegroom, not his own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See the friend of the Bridegroom saying, “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He, for whom thou art suffering, was not the friend of the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast not the wedding garment; and if thou art come to the feast, thou wilt be put out of doors; nay, thou hast been cast out of doors already, and for that reason thou art wretched: return at length, and do not boast.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what the apostle says: “Though I should distribute all my goods to the poor, and give my body to be burnt, but have not charity.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See what thou dost not have.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Though,” he saith, “I should give my body to be burnt;” and that, too, for the name of Christ; but since there are many who do this boastfully, not with charity, therefore, “Though I should give my body to be burnt, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was by charity those martyrs, who suffered in time of persecution, did this; but these men do it of their vanity and pride; for in the absence of a persecutor, they throw themselves headlong into destruction.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Come, then, that thou mayest have charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But we have our martyrs.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What martyrs?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are not doves; hence they attempted to fly, and fell over the rock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: You see then, my brethren, that all things cry against them, all the divine pages, all prophecy, the whole gospel, all the apostolic letters, every sigh of the dove, and yet they awake not, they do not yet rouse from their sleep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we are the dove, let us groan, let us persevere, let us hope; God’s compassion will be with you, that the fire of the Holy Spirit may glow in your simplicity; and they will come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There must be no despairing; pray, preach, love; the Lord is able to the utmost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Already they begin to be sensible of their shame; many have become sensible of it, and blushed; Christ will aid, that the rest also may become sensible of it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: However, my brethren, at least let the chaff alone remain there; let all the grain be gathered together; let whatever has borne fruit among them return to the ark by the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Failing everywhere else, what do they now allege against us, not finding what to say?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They have taken away our houses, they have taken away our estates.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They bring forward wills.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “See, Gaius Seius made a grant of an estate to the church over which Faustinus presided.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of what church was Faustinus bishop?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is the church?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the church over which Faustinus presided, said he.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But Faustinus presided not over a church, but over a sect.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The dove, however, is the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why cry out?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have not devoured houses; let the dove have them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let inquiry be made who the dove is, and let her have them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For you know, my brethren, that those houses of theirs are not Augustin’s; and if you know it not, and imagine that I delight in the possession of them, God knows, yea, knows my judgment respecting those estates, and even what I suffer in that matter; He knows my groaning, since He has deigned to impart to me somewhat of the dove.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, there are those estates; by what right dost thou assert thy claim to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By divine right, or by human?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them answer: Divine right we have in the Scriptures, human right in the laws of kings.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By what right does every man possess what he possesses?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it not by human right?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For by divine right, “The earth is the Lord’s, and the fullness thereof.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The poor and the rich God made of one clay; the same earth supports alike the poor and the rich.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By human right, however, one says, This estate is mine, this house is mine, this servant is mine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By human right, therefore, is by right of the emperors.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because God has distributed to mankind these very human rights through the emperors and kings of this world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do you wish us to read the laws of the emperors, and to act by the estates according to these laws?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you will have your possession by human right, let us recite the laws of the emperors; let us see whether they would have the heretics possess anything.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is the emperor to me? thou sayest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is by right from him that thou possessest the land.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or take away rights created by emperors, and then who will dare say, That estate is mine, or that slave is mine, or this house is mine?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, however, in order to their possessing these things, men have received rights derived from kings, will ye that we read the laws, that you may be glad in having even a single garden, and impute it to nothing but the clemency of the dove that you are permitted to remain in possession even there?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there are to be read well known laws, in which the emperors have directed that those who, being outside the communion of the Catholic Church, usurp to themselves the name of Christians, and are not willing in peace to worship the Author of peace, may not dare to possess anything in the name of the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what have we to do with the emperor?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But I have already said that we are treating of human right.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet the apostle would have us obey kings, would have us honor kings, and said, “Honor the king.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not say, What have I to do with the king? as in that case, what have you to do with the possession?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is by the rights derived from kings that possessions are enjoyed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast said, What have I to do with the king?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Say not then that the possessions are thine; because it is to those same human rights, by which men enjoy their possessions, thou hast referred them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is with divine right I have to do, saith he.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, let us read the Gospel; let us see how far extends the Catholic Church of Christ, upon whom the dove came, which taught, “This is He that baptizeth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In what way, then, can he possess by divine right, who says, “I baptize;” whilst the dove says, “This is He that baptizeth;” whilst the Scripture says, “My dove is one, the only one of her mother”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why have you torn the dove?—nay, rather, have torn your own bowels, for while you are yourselves torn to pieces, the dove continues entire.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, if, driven from every point, they have nothing to say, I will tell them what to do; let them come to the Catholic Church, and together with us, they will have not only the earth, but Him also who made heaven and earth.
